name: Aditya Parikh
headline: Machine Learning Researcher | PhD Student | Generalist
affiliation: Department of Applied Mathematics and Computer Science, Technical University of Denmark (DTU Compute)
bio: Optimizing not only for performance but also for fairness, generalizability and robustness. I'm interested in building frameworks that expose when standard evaluation/metrics, which we often trust blindly. I'm into a fast-moving, cross-disciplinary environments because I believe the most important questions rarely sit neatly in one field.
currentWorkSummary: Current work centers on responsible medical AI: evaluating and improving clinical reliability of vision and vision-language models under real-world bias and distribution shift.
currentWorkItems: Analyzing decoding behavior, saliency alignment, and clinical terminology fidelity in report generation.
Studying fairness across age, gender, and ethnicity with robust subgroup evaluation protocols.

education.focus: Thesis: Representation Learning for Laparoscopic Imaging (Grade: 1.7)

workExperience.summary: Built VLM and weak/single-positive learning pipelines for medical imaging (report-to-image generation, CVM classification, CheXpert-style labels), improving accuracy and reducing annotation cost.
Designed encoder-decoder segmentation frameworks with organ-specific decoders and knowledge sharing for laparoscopic imaging under class imbalance.
Developed multimodal depression predictive model using facial and physiological signals to identify facial expression attributes linked to clinical depression.
Developed adaptive filtering methods for non-stationary automotive acoustic echo cancellation, improving early convergence by ~35% and runtime efficiency by 12%.
Built a school attendance pipeline achieving 97%+ accuracy across varied lighting/poses, robust to camera angle.
Co-designed an AI-powered document processing system that reduced manual entry time by 10x for Indian population census data (1950–2010).

highlightedWorks.title: Clinical Terminology Erasure in Radiology VLMs
highlightedWorks.description: Shows how conventional generation metrics can miss clinically meaningful terminology drops.

highlightedWorks.title: Label Bias and Age-Disparity Analysis
highlightedWorks.description: Visualizes subgroup disparity sources and representational shifts in segmentation performance.

highlightedWorks.title: Fair Lung Disease Diagnosis with Adversarial Attention
highlightedWorks.description: Highlights concept-level balancing strategies for chest CT classification and fairness.

photography.title: Presenting at ISBI
photography.caption: Sharing ongoing research on label bias and age-related performance disparities with the medical imaging community.

photography.title: Radiology Report Generation Validation
photography.caption: Investigating textual metrics vs clinical terminology retention in modern vision-language decoder pipelines.

photography.title: Concept-Level Balancing Experiments
photography.caption: Visualizing gender-adversarial attention maps to ensure fair predictive distributions across protected groups.
